articles.title: Flow for TypeScript Users in 2026
articles.description: Flow is Meta's mature typed dialect of JavaScript, and over the years its syntax has converged closely with TypeScript's. This post walks through where the two now differ: Flow's stricter defaults reject several crash-prone patterns TypeScript's strict mode accepts, and it adds features of its own, like exhaustive match expressions.
articles.author: George Zahariev (Meta)

articles.title: npm v12 to Stop Running Install Scripts by Default
articles.description: The forthcoming npm v12 will no longer execute preinstall/install/postinstall scripts, unless you explicitly allow them. You can get ready now by using npm 11.16.0 which prints warnings about anything v12 would block.
articles.author: GitHub

articles.title: 🤖 Code is Cheap(er)
articles.description: The creator of htmx says that while code is increasingly easy to produce, understanding is still expensive, and “complexity remains our apex predator.”
articles.author: Carson Gross

articles.title: How TanStack Cut TypeScript Type-Checking Work by Up to 86%
articles.description: TanStack Table v9 (now in beta) made its features modular, but the generic types behind that flexibility introduced noticeable editor lag in alpha. This deep-dive shows how the team used tsc diagnostics to cut type-checking work by 62–86%, with lessons for anyone authoring type-heavy libraries.
articles.author: Kevin Van Cott (TanStack)

articles.title: 🛠️ React Libraries and Tools for 2026
articles.description: Every year, Robin updates this post covering his opinionated list of essential React ecosystem libraries.
articles.author: Robin Wieruch

articles.title: Changing the Kindle's Homepage by Editing Hermes Bytecode
articles.description: The Kindle’s UI now runs on a Hermes-powered JavaScript stack. Here’s how you can edit the bytecode Hermes produces to change the Kindle UI’s behavior.
articles.author: Nicholas Tarta

articles.title: 📈 Making numpy-ts as Fast as Native
articles.description: How a TypeScript NumPy port went from 15x slower to matching native, by changing not whether to use WASM, but who owns the bytes.
articles.author: Nico Dupont

articles.title: eslint-plugin-unicorn 67.0: More Than 200 Powerful ESLint Rules
articles.description: Sindre’s set of curated rules has grown a lot, with almost 100 added or updated in the past few weeks alone. Like enforcing better comments, limiting the depth of nested calls, preferring Temporal over Date, and preferring HTTPS over HTTP in URLs. These rules will seriously challenge (and improve!) your code.
articles.author: Sindre Sorhus et al.

articles.title: zod-compiler: Compile Zod Schemas into Zero-Overhead Validators
articles.description: A build-time compiler that plugs into Vite, webpack, et al. and rewrites existing schemas into optimized, tree-shakeable validators. Claims 2–75x faster validation with no source changes and the full Zod API preserved.
articles.author: Gajus Kuizinas

articles.title: 📊 billboard.js 4.0: A JavaScript Chart Library Based on D3
articles.description: At almost nine years old, billboard.js now has a new, opt-in canvas-based rendering mode (SVG remains the default), smaller bundles, and better performance on large datasets. There are hundreds of demos ranging from donut charts and funnel charts to overlapping bar charts and radar charts.
articles.author: Jae Sung Park (NAVER)

articles.title: PolyCSS: CSS 3D Engine for the DOM
articles.description: Renders textured 3D polygon meshes entirely in the DOM using CSS matrix3d() transforms — no WebGL or <canvas> required. Works with vanilla JS, React, and Vue. There’s a gallery to explore and a live builder if you want to play around.
articles.author: LayoutitStudio

articles.description: The pnpm team has created a new GitHub Action to set up both pnpm and a JS runtime (Node, Deno, or Bun).
🐈 Compilecat (repo) is a new, experimental JavaScript performance compiler that does Closure-style optimizations.
Several major projects are taking shape in beta/pre-release stages right now, including SvelteKit 3.0, Vue 3.6, Vite 8.1, and Astro 7.0.
Playwright 1.61 – You can now register and test passkeys, as well as read/write to localStorage and sessionStorage via a new WebStorage API.
ESLint v10.5.0 – Five core rules now highlight smaller ranges of code to avoid shadowing other problems in editors.
🤖 TanStack AI Beta – Framework and provider-agnostic AI toolkit.

articles.title: Introducing the MDN MCP Server
articles.description: Bring MDN’s docs and browser compatibility data straight into an AI agent or IDE for accurate, up-to-date answers about the web platform, instead of relying on models' stale training data.
articles.author: The MDN Team

articles.title: The Scope of CSS @function
articles.description: A look at the evaluation scope of CSS custom functions: unlike regular custom properties that inherit through the DOM, they resolve variables from wherever they're called, letting library authors hide complex logic behind clean APIs, with some caveats.
articles.author: Jane Ori

articles.title: The Golden Rule of Customizable Select
articles.description: When using Safari 27's new customizable <select> element, always keep text on your option elements. Skipping that in favor of just icons or swatches hurts usability, accessibility, and older-browser fallbacks.
articles.author: Yitbarek & Nguyen (WebKit)

articles.title: Why Isn't My 3D View Transition Working?
articles.description: Short answer: the view transition pseudo tree has no real parent, so the perspective property has nothing to latch onto. This post walks through the fix.
articles.author: Sunkanmi Fafowora

articles.title: Demystifying the View Transition Pseudo Tree
articles.description: Breaks down each layer of a pseudo-element tree that a browser builds during a view transition, looking at what each pseudo-element does, why they’re there, and how to target them when defaults need tweaking.
articles.author: Cyd Stumpel

articles.title: ▶  Get Started with the <model> Element
articles.description: The model element brings interactive 3D content into the page on iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS.
articles.author: Aleksei Marchenko (Apple Developer)

articles.title: Creating Memorable Web Experiences: A Modern CSS Toolkit
articles.description: A practical reminder and tooling overview, looking at what we can now achieve without the technical overhead of the past.
articles.author: Mariana Beldi

articles.title: Improvements to Web for AI Should Benefit All Users
articles.description: The Safari team recently opposed the WebMCP API, and Jason argues that rather than building AI-specific APIs, we should fix the underlying semantics. He concludes: “user needs come before agent needs”.
articles.author: Jason Grigsby

articles.title: Prop For That: What JS Knows, Now CSS Knows
articles.description: A library to tackle areas where CSS almost gets you there, but you’re forced to drop into JavaScript to get a value: mouse position, the time, a slider’s value, etc. Add an HTML attribute and the live values show up as custom properties, bridging the JS–CSS gap declaratively. It has a great demo page.
articles.author: Adam Argyle

articles.title: Mitos ASCII Tool: Convert Images into ASCII Text Illustrations
articles.description: Used at Oxide for creating branded ASCII graphics, this tool not only converts images into ASCII art, but lets you live-code effects for animated hero image experiences, etc. GitHub repo.
articles.author: Oxide Computer Company

articles.title: Discover MapKit JS 6: Rebuilt for Today’s Web Developer
articles.description: Apple’s privacy-first framework for integrating Maps into your site has had a big update. Version six ships as an npm package, and introduces TypeScript support via DefinitelyTyped. There’s a demo here.
articles.author: Tim Chien (WebKit)

articles.title: Templatical: An Open Source, Drag-and-Drop Email Editor SDK
articles.description: Supports custom blocks with API-backed data, merge tags with your choice of template literals, full theming via design tokens, and lots more. The playground includes real-world use cases. Source.
articles.author: Templatical

articles.title: Svelte Animations: 50+ Free Animated Components and Effects Made with Svelte, Motion SV, and Tailwind
articles.description: Includes three different component packs (Magic, Spell, and Fancy), which you can try out here.
articles.author: Sikandar Bhide
